summary: McKenzie uses an extended metaphor of cows, milk, cookies, and pails to explain his conversion strategy for Bingo Card Creator. Rather than trying to sell software directly to people searching for bingo card software, he attracts teachers searching for free classroom activities (cookies), then guides them through increasingly valuable free offerings until they hit a natural limit that requires purchase. The strategy targets users who don't know they need his product by solving their immediate problem first, then revealing the paid solution at the exact moment of need. He argues this approach will generate far more conversions than marketing to people already shopping for bingo card software.
key_points: Free samples should attract people searching for solutions to their actual problem, not people shopping for your product category
The conversion funnel should escalate commitment gradually: free web sample → free download → purchase, with each step proving value before asking for more
The paywall should appear at the exact moment of demonstrated need, not before the user understands the product's value
Most potential customers don't know your product category exists, so market the problem you solve rather than the solution you sell
money_quotes: We're all sitting by the side of the road with a big sign saying 'Get your free milk!' Sometimes somebody ambles down, sees our sign, and says 'Sure, I'll take some free milk.' And then he drinks it and leaves before we can even tell him how well-tempered of a cow she is.
I am not in the business of giving away milk, or selling cows. My philosophy with Bingo Card Creator is that I give away cookies.
Few people get up in the morning and say 'I think I'll go take a stroll around the neighborhood to find an independent farmer to buy milk from'.
Put up a sign saying free cookies and you'll be out of them before you can say boo.
She came to my site because I offer free activities to do exactly what she wants to do.
She's got the proof literally in her hands by this point if she printed the PDFs.
I anticipate this process will generate a heck of a lot more conversions over time than actually selling to people who know they're in the market for 'make my own printable bingo cards'.
key_insight: The most effective conversion strategy attracts users searching for solutions to their immediate problem, delivers escalating free value to build trust, then reveals the purchase option at the precise moment of demonstrated need.
